rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM PEARSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Stay
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, AUGUST 29, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.¿s motion to stay is off calendar pursuant to representation by defendant that the conflict issue has been resolved. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY PETERSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS
rulings.Rulings: LINE 4, Deft Cahill Construction Co., Inc.¿s MSJ is denied and its alternate MSA is denied as to premises owner/contractor liability and off calendar as to negligence (survival and wrongful death), products liability (survival and wrongful death), false representation and punitive damages. Deft failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that pltfs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Peterson was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Pltfs¿ responses to deft¿s special interrogatories preclude deft from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Specifically, pltfs¿ responses identify Mr. Cantley as a person with knowledge of Mr. Peterson¿s exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to deft, state what knowledge he has and how he has it, and deft failed to demonstrate that he has no such knowledge. Pltfs have dismissed their causes of action for negligence (survival and wrongful death), products liability (survival and wrongful death), false representation and their claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only 1 reporter.There will be only 1 official record. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to B&P Code, Sec. 8016. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY PETERSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS
rulings.Rulings: LINE 4, Defendant Cosco Fire Protection, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and/or summary adjudication is denied on two grounds. First, defendant¿s motion is untimely. Defendant¿s proof of service demonstrates that it failed to provide proper notice as required by C.C.P. sections 437c and 1010.6. Plaintiffs¿ opposition argued that the motion was untimely and thus plaintiffs did not waive such argument. Second, although not mentioned anywhere in defendant¿s moving papers, this is the second motion for summary judgment and/or summary adjudication defendant has brought in this matter. Defendant failed to demonstrate that the proper circumstances exist to bring a second motion for summary judgment and/or summary adjudication. Defendant failed to demonstrate that new or different facts, circumstances or law warrant this second motion pursuant to either C.C.P. section 1008(b) or C.C.P. section 437c(f)(2). Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $1000. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter. There will be only one official record. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND GENE BENNETT et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: LINE 2, Defendant Oakfabco, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Bennett was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to deft. The court only considered the evidence submitted with plaintiffs¿ initial opposition. The court could not consider the evidence submitted with plaintiffs¿ supplemental opposition because it was untimely pursuant to C.C.P. section 437c and pltfs failed to demonstrate good cause for the late filing and service of their supplemental opposition and all attached papers. (See Hobson v. Raychem Corp. (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 614, 623-625 (disapproved on other grounds in Colmenares v. Braemar Country Club, Inc. (2003) 29 Cal.4th 1019).) Defendant¿s objections to the untimely supplemental opposition are sustained. Defendant¿s objections to A.P. Green¿s interrogatory responses are sustained. The court did not consider any of the deposition testimony of Mr. Bennett. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one reporter. There will be only one official record. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to B&P Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Rulings: LINE 3, Defendant Kaiser Foundation Hospitals¿ motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the claim for loss of consortium and granted as to the claim for punitive damages. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Patterson was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Patterson¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong. As to punitive damages, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only 1 reporter. There will be only 1 official record. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to B&P Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on 1 reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring it to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
